It also favors a chronological approach rather than a topical one. The SBOE had narrowed the options for a new framework to one supported by the conservative Texas Public Policy Foundation and another backed by the progressive Texas Council for Social Studies. While the latter prevailed in a preliminary vote, members ultimately . The new framework lays a foundation of "Stories of America and Texas" in kindergarten through second grade, followed by on Western civilization, U.S. history, and Texas history in third through eighth grade. After the vote, the Texas Public Policy Foundation said the new framework "ensures that our kids will learn the true story of freedom: where it began, how it grew, and how Texas stands as its greatest embodiment." Stray Dogs Terrorize Neighborhood A pack of stray dogs is prowling around a Godley subdivision in Johnson County, and officials say they . Alarmed residents have shared videos showing up to 40 dogs at a time roaming the neighborhood. The dogs are believed to have . Last night's city council agenda called for discussion on a proposal to increase penalties for dumping animals. Neither the city nor the county has an animal shelter. "There is not a solution at this time ," said Precinct 1 Commissioner Rick Bailey. Some residents worry it is only a matter of time before one of the dogs . "They’re feral and they're aggressive, and I don’t want them near any children whatsoever ," said Heather Figueroa. "We now have dogs... that are out and about at the same time that my children and our children are on the streets ," . "The dogs are going to look for food, and I don’t want my children to be that dog food ," he added. Frisco Teen Named Kid of the Year Tejasvi Manoj, a student at Frisco ISD's Lebanon Trail High School, has been named TIME 's for creating an app to help seniors identify phishing scams and other cybercrimes. After her grandfather to a $2,000 debt repayment scam last year, Manoj learned that similar scams cost nearly 860,000 victims over $16 billion in losses each year. Having participated in programs like Girls Who Code, she developed an app to address the problem. Currently in , Shield Seniors identifies scams by to analyze suspicious emails and texts. It includes a chatbot, a tool to upload screenshots, and a guide for reporting fraud. Since developing her app, Manoj has also received an honorable mention in last year's Congressional App Challenge, in Plano, and visited assisted living facilities to talk with residents about staying safe online. | Flying together with our sponsor Scientists discovered that men with a severely inflamed prostate have this ONE thing in common... subject: Portland City Council Passes Toothless Resolution Condemning ICE
body:
Here's the latest breaking news at The Maine Wire

** Portland City Council Passes Toothless Resolution Condemning ICE (https://mainepolicy.us9.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a975bf67c40a8d8be91ad606d&id=2d92834445&e=f093fa355c)
------------------------------------------------------------
By Seamus Othot on Sep 17, 2025 06:53 am
Amid a slew of obscure and symbolic agenda items, such as proclamations recognizing “Diaper Need Awareness Week” and “Hispanic Heritage Month,” the Portland City Council slipped in another item at its Monday meeting that did not appear on the agendas — one condemning Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) practices. [RELATED: ICE Arrests Illegal Immigrant Outside [...]

The post Portland City Council Passes Toothless Resolution Condemning ICE (https://mainepolicy.us9.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a975bf67c40a8d8be91ad606d&id=36b0269b77&e=f093fa355c) appeared first on The Maine Wire (https://mainepolicy.us9.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a975bf67c40a8d8be91ad606d&id=d6bdfa6f90&e=f093fa355c) .
Read the full story » (https://mainepolicy.us9.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a975bf67c40a8d8be91ad606d&id=5ea27a4ae7&e=f093fa355c)

** Maine Ranked One of the Worst States for Teachers: WalletHub Report (https://mainepolicy.us9.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a975bf67c40a8d8be91ad606d&id=eb0c673ea7&e=f093fa355c)
------------------------------------------------------------

name: The Turning Point USA Family
subject: Charlie Kirk, 1993–2025
body:
#
[https://secure.anedot.com/turning-point-usa/2e7c1ee5-5f6b-41f8-81ae-eeaffbbbb106?source_code=MAWCO69572&utm_medium=email&utm_source=hardask_acq&utm_campaign=ck_legacy&utm_content=ab_test_a&utm_term=MAWCO69572]

Friend,

Charlie Kirk didn’t just live a full life...

He showed us what it meant to live boldly, love deeply, and lead with
truth.

With every ounce of his strength, he worked tirelessly to wake up a
generation... To tell the truth... To save our country... And, above
all, to point people back to God.

And in the end — _Charlie gave it all._

No one can replace him. But all of us can carry forward with what he
started.

Patriot, we want to give you a glimpse into the legacy he left behind
— _and the calling that now belongs to us._

WE MADE THIS TRIBUTE TO HONOR THE MAN WHO GAVE EVERYTHING... AND TO
REMIND THE WORLD THAT HIS MISSION WILL PRESS ON:

WATCH OUR TRIBUTE TO CHARLIE HERE
[https://secure.anedot.com/turning-point-usa/2e7c1ee5-5f6b-41f8-81ae-eeaffbbbb106?source_code=MAWCO69572&utm_medium=email&utm_source=hardask_acq&utm_campaign=ck_legacy&utm_content=ab_test_a&utm_term=MAWCO69572]

All of us will miss Charlie. _None of us will ever forget him._

For Charlie,

name: Craig Riedel for State Senate
subject: Riedel Campaign Update
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Craig Riedel
body:
| | | | |
---
Riedel Campaign Update | September 17, 2025
---
Riedel Issues Statement on Charlie Kirk
---
|
---
"I was shocked and heart broken to hear of the assassination of Charlie Kirk yesterday. Charlie was a husband, father, and patriot who inspired a generation. As we all mourn this incredible loss, we must not back down from the values that Charlie held and fought for everyday. Please join Danette and I in continuing to pray for Charlie's family and our nation."
On Wednesday, September 10, Charlie Kirk's life was taken because he fought for the values that our nation was founded on: faith, family and freedom. This was not only a fatal attack on Charlie, but also on the soul of our nation. In the face of this tragedy, conservatives have come together across the country to hold vigils and memorials to honor Charlie's legacy. In less than one week since the attack, Turning Point USA has received more than 54,000 requests to start or join a Turning Point Chapter in communities all across the country.
"You intended to harm me, but God intended it for good to accomplish what is now being done, the saving of many lives." (Genesis 50:20, NIV)
---
|
---
County Fair Season Wraps up in Northwest Ohio With fair season officially over in District 1, Craig and Danette are happy to say they visited each of the 10 county fairs several times. Over the past month, Craig made it to the Defiance, Hancock, Van Wert, Fulton, Hardin and Williams County fairs to both volunteer and talk with voters. Craig took part in the Fulton County veteran's lunch where he helped serve food and thanked every veteran for their service. At the Williams County fair, Craig volunteered his time in the Pork Producer's stand where he helped serve food to fair-goers.
---
| | |
---
|
---
| | |
---
| |
---
| Craig & Danette Host Defiance Fundraiser

Craig and Danette hosted their first campaign fundraiser this month in Defiance. Many Defiance County locals came to show their enthusiasm for Craig's campaign. Craig was grateful and encouraged from the outpouring of support that he and Danette received.
---
|
---
| |
---
| |
---
| Riedel Campaign Announces Upcoming Fundraisers
The Riedel campaign will be hosting several additional fundraisers this fall, with the next two coming in October. The next fundraiser will be held in Indian Lake, with a fundraiser in Findlay to follow. Craig and Danette are very excited for the momentum and support already building behind these upcoming fundraisers.
---
| | |
---
| | |
---
| |
---
|
# Parade Season in District 1

As parade season continues, Craig and Danette haven't missed a beat. Over the past month, they've enjoyed walking in parades in Putnam, Van Wert, and Paulding Counties. After the parades, Craig and Danette like to stick around and experience the festivals that follow.
---
| |
---

---
| |
---

---
| |
---

---
| |
---
| Craig Speaks at 9/11 Memorial Ceremony

Craig was honored to participate in the Defiance Firefighters Local 918 9/11 Memorial Service. Craig spoke on the impacts and aftermath of the day that shook our nation 24 years ago. Craig also spent time commemorating the firefighters and first responders that immediately stepped into the line of duty. These brave men and women showed what it truly means to be an American.
---
| |
---
| |
---

clean_body: | | | | | Riedel Campaign Update | September 17, 2025 Riedel Issues Statement on Charlie Kirk | "I was shocked and heart broken to hear of the assassination of Charlie Kirk yesterday. Charlie was a husband, father, and patriot who inspired a generation. As we all mourn this incredible loss, we must not back down from the values that Charlie held and fought for everyday. Please join Danette and I in continuing to pray for Charlie's family and our nation." On Wednesday, September 10, Charlie Kirk's life was taken because he fought for the values that our nation was founded on: faith, family and freedom. This was not only a fatal attack on Charlie, but also on the soul of our nation. In the face of this tragedy, conservatives have come together across the country to hold vigils and memorials to honor Charlie's legacy. In less than one week since the attack, Turning Point USA has received more than 54,000 requests to start or join a Turning Point Chapter in communities all across the country. "You intended to harm me, but God intended it for good to accomplish what is now being done, the saving of many lives." (Genesis 50:20, NIV) | County Fair Season Wraps up in Northwest Ohio With fair season officially over in District 1, Craig and Danette are happy to say they visited each of the 10 county fairs several times. Over the past month, Craig made it to the Defiance, Hancock, Van Wert, Fulton, Hardin and Williams County fairs to both volunteer and talk with voters. Craig took part in the Fulton County veteran's lunch where he helped serve food and thanked every veteran for their service. At the Williams County fair, Craig volunteered his time in the Pork Producer's stand where he helped serve food to fair-goers. | | | | | | | | | | Craig & Danette Host Defiance Fundraiser Craig and Danette hosted their first campaign fundraiser this month in Defiance. Many Defiance County locals came to show their enthusiasm for Craig's campaign. Craig was grateful and encouraged from the outpouring of support that he and Danette received. | | | | | | Riedel Campaign Announces Upcoming Fundraisers The Riedel campaign will be hosting several additional fundraisers this fall, with the next two coming in October. The next fundraiser will be held in Indian Lake, with a fundraiser in Findlay to follow. Craig and Danette are very excited for the momentum and support already building behind these upcoming fundraisers. | | | | | | | | | Parade Season in District 1 As parade season continues, Craig and Danette haven't missed a beat. Over the past month, they've enjoyed walking in parades in Putnam, Van Wert, and Paulding Counties. After the parades, Craig and Danette like to stick around and experience the festivals that follow. | | | | | | | | | Craig Speaks at 9/11 Memorial Ceremony Craig was honored to participate in the Defiance Firefighters Local 918 9/11 Memorial Service. Craig spoke on the impacts and aftermath of the day that shook our nation 24 years ago. Craig also spent time commemorating the firefighters and first responders that immediately stepped into the line of duty. These brave men and women showed what it truly means to be an American. | | | | | Paid for by Friends of Craig Riedel | | == | | == | == == | | | | Paid for by Friends of Craig Riedel | 1246 Hilton Head Ct. | Defiance, OH 43512 US | | | | | | |

body:
Let’s ruin Justice Barrett’s visit and ensure that we don’t lost Pennsylvania’s Supreme Court like we’ve lost SCOTUS.
| | | | | [](https://yfq78zhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001kYZRYvt3Fzc39PSy2jA8lnEpaukuSGkLtWFUY4qIKcWaOvW5a81JoXYTtromvldtCtmPX8bF3lp_pi56MnazrO33CDRYMMjQ-d09E5At58ze8T81uIhSTSIx3BwF4_1PabGHKo4crENMwGlNzVgDsj-jKK5OzYaGchXGeG6Zei6qUsuXSeDagZAyQUnHLHmArnf-Ri7AkMPeub6t2a9a0pwvHV9nXAMu0hyfd1b674I=&c=jE92W5bnIT9ixtTc52R6b3zJsW2KrlTaHo7-VlNut3EtkXfJl9o80g==&ch=MxcfGt6BLoK9Uh3NFyoVo_fdhOF2ebB-jS6JlX9RPFRUTlQj0YN1gg==)

| Peter,
Today is Constitution Day and right-wing Supreme Court Justice Amy Coney Barrett is headed to Philadelphia to market her new book at the National Constitution Center.
Looming over her visit is the spectre of MAGA's all out effort to capture Pennsylvania's very own State Supreme Court in an obscure judicial election this November. If MAGA pulls off a win here it means Democrats never winning PA again - 19 electoral college votes gone forever.
Justice Barrett and the sinister direction our national Supreme Court is taking the nation is the embodiment of what is at stake here in Pennsylvania this November. This is why Defend Our Courts is holding a massive rally to spoil Justice Barrett's visit and galvanize the Democratic effort to save PA this Fall.

| | [](https://yfq78zhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001kYZRYvt3Fzc39PSy2jA8lnEpaukuSGkLtWFUY4qIKcWaOvW5a81JoXYTtromvldtgkJ1KjJ7V615_IoImveCiNqF4i7PTQsOCoDrT5GQbrN3sWu4SJ9D52mhy5N0LZSFR_ML04OqtjjXWyV03V3OxiU9_9S8heSUX9fJwrzhln3ckVDg2xcDD7ZTk7kMeHdhP0Yj41g-fN5DmZlgWsH3i4JJrgA8jWSkdZ7BS2qm8pQbB44Rwnia5pPlcc7bGqCoXtg48ketNkaegjvaIqB_XFWRgfLdRgvAaMXQXDTcbx9C5KV5il9GUu7Q6gOW_9vk&c=jE92W5bnIT9ixtTc52R6b3zJsW2KrlTaHo7-VlNut3EtkXfJl9o80g==&ch=MxcfGt6BLoK9Uh3NFyoVo_fdhOF2ebB-jS6JlX9RPFRUTlQj0YN1gg==)

We are going all out to disrupt her visit. We'll have free food, a big brass band, special guest speakers, street puppets of Donald Trump, Elon Musk, and Putin, and other fun surprises.
Given how massive this rally will be, it will be a great time for our organizers to recruit new volunteers, register voters, and ensure the rally's attendees know about the upcoming November 2025 Supreme Court election.
[If you are in Pennsylvania and are able to attend the rally which will begin at 5:30 PM ET in front of the National Constitution Center you can sign up here.](https://yfq78zhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001kYZRYvt3Fzc39PSy2jA8lnEpaukuSGkLtWFUY4qIKcWaOvW5a81JoXYTtromvldtgkJ1KjJ7V615_IoImveCiNqF4i7PTQsOCoDrT5GQbrN3sWu4SJ9D52mhy5N0LZSFR_ML04OqtjjXWyV03V3OxiU9_9S8heSUX9fJwrzhln3ckVDg2xcDD7ZTk7kMeHdhP0Yj41g-fN5DmZlgWsH3i4JJrgA8jWSkdZ7BS2qm8pQbB44Rwnia5pPlcc7bGqCoXtg48ketNkaegjvaIqB_XFWRgfLdRgvAaMXQXDTcbx-0GsOYpsicb0VghthcvI8_&c=jE92W5bnIT9ixtTc52R6b3zJsW2KrlTaHo7-VlNut3EtkXfJl9o80g==&ch=MxcfGt6BLoK9Uh3NFyoVo_fdhOF2ebB-jS6JlX9RPFRUTlQj0YN1gg==)

Patriot - I often think about my father's journey to America. He arrived with just $1.75 in his pocket and a dream in his heart. It's a reminder that the American Dream is within reach, no matter where we start.

As I run for Governor of Virginia, I'm reminded of the power of small actions adding up to make a big difference. That's why I'm writing to ask for your help today.

_**If every person reading this email could give just $1.75, we would hit our major fundraising goal and be one step closer to winning this toss-up race.**_ It's a small ask, but it would make a huge impact.

You see, my father's story isn't just about him - it's about the opportunities that America provides. It's about the chance to build a better life, to work hard, and to succeed. And that's exactly what I'm fighting for.

As a Marine, a businesswoman, and a public servant, I've seen firsthand the positive impact that conservative policies can have on our communities. I've fought for education reform, economic growth, and individual freedom. _**And I'm just getting started.**_

[**So I'm asking you to join me in this fight. Will you give $1.75 today to help us reach our goal? It may seem like a small amount, but trust me, it will add up to make a big difference.**](http://links.conservativesdefendingfreedom.com/u/click?_t=91d01aa68bbe4105984439520ca0f0af&_m=ba455f6a19fe4a4594d802b8fa74a33c&_e=QVPtYsuq5enqOPU91NGh4otdKvjLQziD0Q96VjWCwHewWN_O_iZijFORy4Ykbp5WQnlDkJ6k68wL6_cVQWEpRO5688OuuzGKRQ7lfPIIMNnpJHvSF0BQAatT4ZiJ-i42JF-XgxVS_y3bYH0SuOElqMhT71FF_iDTDXfvzm0rGpwZRkQfQNHQzKFAowpNt4bg51rflMfPaAnOzRpbD7QOPPz18OSv4XXCHuIZZW8axJDE_FjMxTfA_tV0RD0cbK2IzY3Ab8GMfsQOv_UH8zIMraFo8bhpGw-4CPm0cNXEuuvSRrFyc-y-GKjhdCBixYEl69GNnIl49kObt6EdNDS9Q6MEQq-coL1eCcBWEZ3zf8aWjqFSaZvwd_fVL3VSS9QxnYXvS2PUrsvpx38WFmWnvGFlVWI2duzhBrJApE9LY5qcU21HwqV-QyA_L1ylGo1vhi5oUJZPFOp3yJ_nMfICarPDT9JunvycvdDLU-3oeua8aUphvKTcpGDOvlZiUpd_b7WbxhEux4tiryHXf5GfdxGcg9X4SKgB-6_o2PkBRPbT70sjEYSTucHhJ2IgGJ85JwiX5mg_KXSsXnjbLcgpn773QjqgEJvYovCQwp8t4O84APkGe69RgyAfaLzpMm5Hr79yiY5xQwYSycMtjoakapoxiE7iVA39bvPwGfntzZ2TRb-0rrqGI1nx7wbbJ_SUeTQs4giZwco5ynUEOQ3gQI8F2QYGC-O6AI4JQ2ATc3k%3D)

Lieutenant Governor.I'm Winsome Earle-Sears, and I've held many titles in my life. Each one hasn't been a cakewalk, but I like a challenge. I've fought for education reform, economic growth, and individual freedom. And I'm just getting started. Together, we can keep the American Dream alive for our kids and grandkids. We can build a brighter future, and we can show the country what it means to lead with conservative values. Thank you for your support, and let's do this! Winsome Earle-Sears . Marine. Mom. Small Business Owner. Lieutenant Governor.I'm Winsome Earle-Sears, and I've held many titles in my life. Each one hasn't been a cakewalk, but I like a challenge. I've tried to show my daughters that what you can accomplish is not decided by the color of your skin or where you were born.Sure, a silver spoon would've made it easier, but I've found a helping hand from your community means so much more. ** This will be another challenge, but with you on my side, I know I can do this. | Paid for by Winsome for Governor Neither military info nor photographs of Winsome Earle-Sears in uniform imply endorsement of Winsome Earle-Sears by the Department of Defense or its particular military departments. PO Box 71596 Richmond, VA 23255-1596

| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/danacriswell/p/discovering-true-greatness?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

| [](https://substack.com/redirect/988eb5f2-6006-47f5-82b3-c70ea1a8f198?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
For years I've dug into the lives of the Founding Fathers. Nearly two decades ago I set out to read a biography of every U.S. president--in order--and I've kept at it. Three men keep drawing me back: George Washington, John Adams, and Benjamin Rush. The more I study them, especially Washington, the more convinced I am that God guided their work.
They accomplished things beyond natural ability: Washington, an untested commander, led a volunteer army to defeat the world's foremost military; Adams was arguably our most principled president, standing his ground on hard questions and moving the nation to his convictions; and Rush was a steady spiritual anchor whose faith never wavered.
These weren't merely "great men." They were what I call **Founding-Father Great**.
This past week--since the murder of Charlie Kirk--I've been asking what truly makes a man great. Why do Washington, Adams, and Rush stand out? And why did Charlie Kirk stand out in our time?
History gives us plenty of notable men--Franklin, Lincoln, Kennedy, and more. But some, like Charlie Kirk, carry an edge of exceptionalism others don't. Why? How? Is that kind of greatness reserved for a chosen few, or can anyone reach it?
Julie and I have been talking about this. What is greatness? Here's my working definition: **Founding-Father Greatness is the quality that propels a man beyond his natural ability.** Elon Musk may be the smartest man alive and he's achieved plenty, but I'm not sure that's the same thing. Natural ability can take you far. Greatness--this kind--goes further than talent.
So is it reserved for a special class, or is it available to everyone? The uncomfortable answer that keeps me up at night: it's available to all of us--including me. The problem isn't access. It's obedience and discipline.
Charlie Kirk actually answered this better than I can. In a campus Q&A, a middle-schooler told Charlie he'd just been saved and asked, what he should do next? Charlie's answer was simple:
1. **Read the Bible every day.**
2. **Do what the Bible tells you.**
3. **Tell others what the Bible says.**

It's so simple it makes us uncomfortable. I wanted some complicated formula for greatness--something conveniently out of reach so I'd have an excuse. But pastors and elders have been saying the same thing for years. The answer is not a secret technique. **The answer is the Holy Spirit --God at work in us through Jesus Christ.**
For me, Charlie's second point is the hardest, though I rarely get past number one. "Do what the Bible tells you" sounds simple until you remember the rich young ruler who was told to sell everything and follow Jesus, or Saul--who hunted Christians--being called to become the Apostle Paul, or Peter leaving the security of a fisherman's life to walk behind the Savior. God's commands can be straightforward but look terrifying.
That's what separates men like Washington, Adams, Rush--and, yes, Charlie Kirk. They didn't just listen for God's direction; they **did** it. Conviction plus self-discipline. Hearing plus obeying.
Charlie accomplished things a college drop-out "shouldn't" accomplish. In his twenties he built and led an organization that shaped a generation and shifted a national conversation. That kind of impact doesn't run on raw IQ or hustle alone. That's the fruit of a man who listened to the Lord and acted when the Spirit said move.
Whoever steps up now--whoever carries the work forward--must be the same kind of man (or woman): filled with the Holy Spirit, reading the Word daily, doing what God says, and unafraid to speak truth. Without following Jesus, no one will reach **Founding-Father Greatness**.
With Him, ordinary people can do extraordinary things.

name: Tucker Carlson
subject: For Charlie, we press on
body:
Our duty now: carry Charlie’s mission forward.
Right now, Charlie would say, "Don't you dare quit"

I don't write emails like this often. But right now? After what happened to Charlie... _I can 't stay quiet._**Charlie Kirk was my friend.** Not just someone I admired, but someone I loved and respected deeply. Losing him was devastating.

In many ways, all of us share in this loss. Charlie fought every day of his life for each one of us... fighting to keep America as great, free, and prosperous. No one I know ever fought for that harder than Charlie.America won't ever be the same. **No one can ever replace him -- but we can make sure his legacy continues on. **_We owe him that._ Because if you knew Charlie, you would know exactly what he would say to us right now: _**"Don 't you dare quit."**_Charlie poured his heart into building a movement to save America. He believed in young people. He believed in ordinary Americans. And he believed that even in our darkest hours, America was the beacon of goodness, freedom, and truth.That's what the Turning Point movement was all about -- and Charlie gave everything he had to that fight.**And our task now is not only to protect what he built -- it's to grow it bigger, stronger, and bolder than anyone ever thought possible. ** More students. More churches. More families. More courage from everyday Americans to stand up and fight for what is right.Turning Point USA was Charlie's vehicle to save America. **He didn 't just start a movement... _he started a revival. _**And now, Friend, we must carry that forward. If you share the same sense of responsibility that I do to honor Charlie's work and press on with his vision...** _Then please join me in keeping his fight alive by making a gift to Turning Point USA today. _** **[Let’s make Turning Point USA the biggest thing that this nation has ever seen... bigger than Charlie ever thought possible. That’s how we honor him best.](http://links.makingamericathegreatest.com/u/click?_t=79fa4a3daf024a0a886c5b498ff32e32&_m=d4accdd3b87a425386ebf75b8a890bb5&_e=iar33-xg-mPwV2kk38L5GuHlTopRRk9pwfV8s4K91Y0RBJjZf5YgycElZxoOuC5iQmnvlKjze2Tm3ozXR-uT4nmLOuz7MkxjFZUgemFFv5_vda5cbHjLA5TbR-QBo54iMawdN9tmopx6zsM6yFR95pdEo7nOBVuO65bAz4X8yaxtEECc-6UpO5wKxCiSbOTEkIuqcM0LXF4cExlqpdRXNpvyoz3BGXnhsby1ijXXvEVtdk4_FPmQXbHOpzUZ6aguJ581zaH7cUpzyPYTg3pYiF9B1OZHOv8WAt60zGgNnKzn9awDjpetfTm6I9wM51zaRLVGmUK_1-7R13Fx4L6BINmzermBW31T3OZILIVaNzBQ4OPkJAsKrnBZm0lm4nFq0Taqa7Xb4dpbconOuNZyehJBuXhAq8UUZT2bUdekRnV9Sh6UteXrOB2Ep6fdb_4NNQRAFfU6oJwvqrHufIN10eMjWn1knP4PXn2YPX1bPnvIhK0NpM0YT030f_8TV03g0R9AhYDRq9c5Vdf1w5Ql5vKKrCpZ4c_HxQdg-6b3cMFS6oeit9Vzgiiy-lMVsfG4DwYAyMvx_Bjh8zmH-w-oUUCIoKq-hqnhloNY8Dp5qnV8SYhJBhpd8sF9xTuzj7a6lRt8ichN3k83Yv6KdmHhigwCnw0ntsVSkdopsMNA7dN2c21OXjn56o_RLIacujlZA1qOGn1Gt2PU6p5ghgop8oK3yHN7UbkTmMCiLYbk7p4%3D)**

I really didn't want to write about Charlie Kirk. But over the last week I've become increasingly shaken by MAGA's attempts use this tragedy to sever our nation's grasp on the existence of an objective reality, and by the willingness of too many Democrats to let them. So I wanted to share this piece, published today in _[The New Republic](https://substack.com/redirect/754ff9e2-4b27-4cb9-9b12-fd2286ab78eb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_. Thanks for reading, and wishing you the best in these bleak, bleak times.
--Aaron
* * *
> _'O 'Brien held up his left hand, its back towards Winston, with the thumb hidden and the four fingers extended._
> _' How many fingers am I holding up, Winston?'_
> _' Four.'_
> _' And if the party says that it is not four but five--then how many?'_
In the days since Charlie Kirk was murdered, I've found myself repeatedly musing on this passage from George Orwell's _1984_. The novel's protagonist, Winston Smith, had once written in his diary, "Freedom is the freedom to say that two plus two make four. If that is granted, all else follows." In Orwell's totalitarian state, this assertion was a thought crime, and following Winston's disappearance into Big Brother's torture chambers, the party sought to break him--or, more specifically, to break his commitment to the existence of an objective reality.
> _' You are a slow learner, Winston,' said O'Brien gently._
> _' How can I help it?' Winston blubbered. 'How can I help seeing what is in front of my eyes? Two and two are four.'_
> _' Sometimes, Winston. Sometimes they are five. Sometimes they are three. Sometimes they are all of them at once. You must try harder. It is not easy to become sane.'_
The moment that news of Kirk's shooting hit the internet, MAGA--its influencers, podcasters, media figures, Republican elected officials, cabinet directors, Vice President JD Vance, and President Donald Trump--immediately began insisting that two and two make five. Their gaslighting around Kirk's death has been so extensive--and so speedily promulgated--that it's hard to fully grasp the sheer magnitude of their mendacity. Here's a very brief summary of some of the self-evidently false assertions the right has been feverishly proclaiming in recent days:
* Kirk's killing was organized by the American left. (The alleged shooter, Tyler Robinson, is a 22-year-old gamer from a conservative Mormon family who did not vote in the 2024 election. All evidence suggests he acted alone.)
* Robinson became indoctrinated by radical leftists while at college. (Robinson attended Utah Valley University--a relatively conservative school whose student body [identifies](https://substack.com/redirect/cafa9328-581b-4b43-b56d-960c2bdacb05?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) as 70% Mormon--for one semester, before enrolling in an electrical engineering program at Utah's Dixie Technical College.)
* The left celebrated Kirk's killing. (Every significant Democratic elected official, including all leading progressives, immediately condemned the shooting and called for an end to political violence.)
* The left is responsible for most political violence in this country. (Unequivocally, the [vast majority](https://substack.com/redirect/5ecab033-6fb7-4b7f-9167-9e26450d876b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) of extremist mass killings in the U.S. are linked to the far right; the [vast majority](https://substack.com/redirect/b179f606-2a53-4332-96c7-33681cfd4948?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) of U.S. political speech praising or inciting violence comes from rightwing politicians; and it sure wasn't a Democratic president that actively instigated a violent attack on the Capitol several years ago.)

Though all of these lies are shocking and dangerous, the narrative that has been most troubling to me--or at least has most caused me to feel like I may actually be losing my mind--has been the right's insistence on the universal public canonization of Kirk. They have endeavored to make his mourning mandatory, enforcing it through threats--backed by the full power of the state--against anyone who dares to share truthful observations of who this man really was. Participate in the hagiographic whitewashing of a dedicated provocateur's career, or suffer the consequences. (Unless, of course, you're Donald Trump, who had already gone back to obsessing over the White House ballroom within hours of Kirk's death, and [skipped](https://substack.com/redirect/5d0de5a8-170f-4009-a1fc-b1437a13c64d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) his Kennedy Center vigil to go golfing.)
Kirk was an extremely talented communicator and activist whose efficacy in building up the organizing infrastructure of the far right has materially affected American politics. He was also, by most reasonable standards, a demagogue whose espousal of [Christian nationalism](https://substack.com/redirect/8c8dd784-71ee-4a05-9144-5301bdba51bd?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), [platforming](https://substack.com/redirect/e023bac2-db5a-458e-b1fb-86e3589d4268?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) of [racist](https://substack.com/redirect/d0e50cd4-4787-4b25-a5f6-9dc3a3844dae?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) [attitudes](https://substack.com/redirect/96f7a640-6816-4b32-9b80-21452c39b3da?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), [attacks](https://substack.com/redirect/52727ae6-82a8-4c4e-a0eb-1965ffb66fd3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) on [free](https://substack.com/redirect/4346c929-2afa-4a10-aa88-e517724030e7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) [speech](https://substack.com/redirect/397e7db5-e1bd-4951-9f17-6ff7d5203551?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), and [support](https://substack.com/redirect/cd0127e2-a192-4d73-80ff-e9a7668ea7f3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) for [political violence](https://substack.com/redirect/d4b4409c-6e3e-4d9f-9caf-558ae8624e53?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) made our country a more dangerous and unjust place.
That doesn't in any way make his murder less vile. Kirk was a human being, and his life was sacred. It is tragic that he was murdered, as it is tragic every time a human is murdered--whether it's a student struck down in their school by another mass shooting, or a civilian shot in their car by a police officer, or a child killed in Gaza by an American-made weapon. We live in a nation where gun deaths are a dime a dozen. For many of us, this is a desperate moral catastrophe and an eternal shame; for [others](https://substack.com/redirect/91aa21c9-a6cd-40fc-83f7-6ebe285d705b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), it's just the price of living in a "free" society.
The effect is that these tragedies are overwhelmingly forgotten within days, hours, or even minutes, as was the case in last week's shooting at Evergreen High School in Colorado, whose victims had the added bad fortune of being killed by a [far-right teen](https://substack.com/redirect/b70d567b-93ee-4de5-ad0c-851a5aa14cfa?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) the same day as Kirk. We barely mourn these tragedies anymore, and--as Kirk himself demonstrated regularly in his [posthumous attacks](https://substack.com/redirect/2692fe43-e3de-4d16-8b28-d15cc0142806?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) on victims of police violence like George Floyd--we certainly do not accept that the circumstances of someone's death rewrite the facts of how they chose to live their lives.
But this rewriting of Kirk's life is being forced on us all the same. Today, Kirk is being framed as an elder statesman and a free speech champion, a model of positive civic virtue. Anyone who resists this false characterization now risks being targeted, not just by MAGA trolls online, but by literally the most powerful people in the world.
Vice President JD Vance, recording "The Charlie Kirk Show" directly from the White House, ended the episode by [exhorting](https://substack.com/redirect/6ef43c44-16a8-482e-892c-3e61bd985969?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) listeners to respond to "someone celebrating Charlie's murder" by "call[ing] their employer." Attorney General Pam Bondi [proclaimed](https://substack.com/redirect/6f52d78f-dc76-45c0-8886-e198b77b2e8f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that employers "have an obligation to get rid of people, you need to look at people who are saying horrible things, and they should not be working with you." She also announced that the DOJ would be looking to prosecute any businesses that fail to support the mourning of Kirk: "Businesses cannot discriminate. If you want to go in and print posters with Charlie's pictures on them for a vigil, you have to let them do that. We can prosecute them for that."
These are not idle threats. Already [dozens](https://substack.com/redirect/f2ceb048-cbef-4a31-a88d-9a926c165541?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) of Americans--teachers, professors, U.S. military members, even a _Washington Post_ columnist--have lost their jobs for sharing messages that were critical of Kirk's stated beliefs and conduct. And far worse free speech crackdowns look to be on the horizon. As White House policy advisor Stephen Miller put it, "With God as my witness, we are going to use every resource we have at the Department of Justice, Homeland Security and throughout this government to identify, disrupt, eliminate and destroy this network and make America safe again for the American people."
It would be bad enough if those of us who are ostensibly opposed to MAGA authoritarianism were facing this assault on the truth with a united front. But we are not. Too many Democratic leaders and liberal pundits have preemptively given in to the far right's framing. The highest-profile example of this was _New York Times_ columnist Ezra Klein's now-infamous [essay](https://substack.com/redirect/1c5136ed-c3db-4188-9483-9d33472a229a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) arguing that Kirk "was practicing politics in exactly the right way." The Kirk of Klein's imagination, a free speech advocate who abhorred political violence, bears very little resemblance to the actual Charlie Kirk, a "Stop the Steal" champion who [sent](https://substack.com/redirect/0c454ab2-40cb-4d31-807f-306ddd586691?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) "80+ buses full of patriots to DC to fight for this president" on January 6th and [encouraged](https://substack.com/redirect/d4b4409c-6e3e-4d9f-9caf-558ae8624e53?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) supporters to bail out the man who attacked Paul Pelosi with a hammer.
Equally shocking were decisions by Democratic governors like [Josh Shapiro](https://substack.com/redirect/d30b3835-5c3b-4523-a10d-445baa19bb1d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and [Jared Polis](https://substack.com/redirect/1038b6f4-1a06-46a4-af32-01d88a89dcd2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to copy President Trump in ordering their state's flags to be lowered to half-staff in honor of Kirk--a sign of respect and public mourning that Polis notably did not extend to the victims at Evergreen High School in his own state. To get a sense of how insane this Democratic folding to the far right is, try imagining the political response we would see if, God forbid, a high-profile leftist like Hasan Piker--who, unlike Kirk, has _not_ worked to actively incite political violence or trafficked in blatant bigotry--were murdered. I find it difficult to imagine Governors Shapiro or Polis engaging in any sort of public mourning for Piker; the idea that any GOP governor would fly their state's flags at half-staff for him is beyond laughable. It's similarly [impossible to imagine](https://substack.com/redirect/e68ac4f7-abab-4a6f-8faa-dbaa76e30c38?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) the Trumpist right enforcing a period of public mourning for a murdered liberal figure--which we saw clearly with their response to the assassination of Minnesota Speaker of the House Melissa Hortman by a Trump-supporting anti-abortion extremist in June..
The affirmation of MAGA's false narratives by high-profile Democrats and liberals greatly reifies the far right's goal of severing our nation's grasp on the existence of an objective reality. Because let's be clear: that is their project. As Winston's torturer in _1984_ put it, "Whatever the Party holds to be the truth, is truth. It is impossible to see reality except by looking through the eyes of the Party. That is the fact that you have got to relearn, Winston." By mobilizing the power of the state to force the nation to see Kirk through the eyes of the Republican Party, the right is using his killing to escalate its war on truth. George Orwell understood the radical dangerousness of such efforts: "The implied objective of this line of thought is a nightmare world in which the Leader, or some ruling clique, controls not only the future, but the past. ... This prospect frightens me much more than bombs."
We cannot allow ourselves, like Klein, Shapiro, and Polis did, to help construct that nightmare world. We must fight to hold onto an understanding of reality that exists outside of MAGA's dictates. As Winston insisted to himself in a moment of clarity, "The solid world exists, its laws do not change. Stones are hard, water is wet, objects unsupported fall towards the earth's centre." Two plus two do not make five. Two plus two make four, and--regardless of the costs--it's never been more important for the enemies of totalitarianism to stand up and say so.

Thanks for reading, and wishing you the best in these bleak, bleak times. --Aaron 'O 'Brien held up his left hand, its back towards Winston, with the thumb hidden and the four fingers extended. ' How many fingers am I holding up, Winston?' ' Four.' ' And if the party says that it is not four but five--then how many?' In the days since Charlie Kirk was murdered, I've found myself repeatedly musing on this passage from George Orwell's 1984. The novel's protagonist, Winston Smith, had once written in his diary, "Freedom is the freedom to say that two plus two make four. If that is granted, all else follows." In Orwell's totalitarian state, this assertion was a thought crime, and following Winston's disappearance into Big Brother's torture chambers, the party sought to break him--or, more specifically, to break his commitment to the existence of an objective reality. ' You are a slow learner, Winston,' said O'Brien gently. ' How can I help it?' Winston blubbered. 'How can I help seeing what is in front of my eyes? Two and two are four.' ' Sometimes, Winston. Sometimes they are five. Sometimes they are three. Sometimes they are all of them at once. You must try harder. It is not easy to become sane.' The moment that news of Kirk's shooting hit the internet, MAGA--its influencers, podcasters, media figures, Republican elected officials, cabinet directors, Vice President JD Vance, and President Donald Trump--immediately began insisting that two and two make five. Their gaslighting around Kirk's death has been so extensive--and so speedily promulgated--that it's hard to fully grasp the sheer magnitude of their mendacity. Here's a very brief summary of some of the self-evidently false assertions the right has been feverishly proclaiming in recent days: Kirk's killing was organized by the American left. (The alleged shooter, Tyler Robinson, is a 22-year-old gamer from a conservative Mormon family who did not vote in the 2024 election. All evidence suggests he acted alone.) Robinson became indoctrinated by radical leftists while at college. (Robinson attended Utah Valley University--a relatively conservative school whose student body as 70% Mormon--for one semester, before enrolling in an electrical engineering program at Utah's Dixie Technical College.) The left celebrated Kirk's killing. (Every significant Democratic elected official, including all leading progressives, immediately condemned the shooting and called for an end to political violence.) * The left is responsible for most political violence in this country. (Unequivocally, the of extremist mass killings in the U.S. are linked to the far right; the of U.S. political speech praising or inciting violence comes from rightwing politicians; and it sure wasn't a Democratic president that actively instigated a violent attack on the Capitol several years ago.) Though all of these lies are shocking and dangerous, the narrative that has been most troubling to me--or at least has most caused me to feel like I may actually be losing my mind--has been the right's insistence on the universal public canonization of Kirk. In fact, the Left is taking yet another victory lap as I write you this email: “Democrats add 1 more vote in Congress after Virginia special election” -Politico We truly do not have any more room for error. We’re ONLY 1 SEAT over the bare minimum threshold to declare a Majority in the House. And so, President Trump and I need to know who we can count on to show up to vote. I trust you’re one of them, Friend. But now let’s make it official. |
### Champion of Free Speech and Faith Charlie Kirk Assassinated
---
| |
---
| The tragic loss of Charlie Kirk is a profound national tragedy that has left us all heartbroken. At just 31 years old, this devoted father leaves behind his wife, Erika, and their two young children, who will grow up without his guidance simply because he dared to voice a differing opinion. Charlie was peacefully seated on stage at Utah Valley University, engaging in an open debate as part of his "American Comeback Tour," when an assassin's bullet ended his life on September 10, 2025, an act of senseless political violence that has shocked the nation. We have been holding his family in our prayers throughout this devastating week, seeking comfort for their unimaginable grief.
Tonight, there will be a community Prayer Vigil in honor of Charlie Kirk at 5 p.m. on the Courthouse Lawn in Goochland County, Virginia. This gathering is an opportunity to come together in unity, reflection, and prayer, echoing the many vigils springing up across the country to remember his legacy.
Charlie Kirk was a tireless advocate for free speech, open debate, and civil discourse. Through Turning Point USA, he created platforms where even his fiercest critics could voice opposing views without fear. Yet, in a heartbreaking act of domestic terrorism, he was assassinated by 22-year-old suspect Tyler Robinson, who reportedly harbored disdain for Kirk's conservative viewpoints. Tragically, following his death, many on the radical left have responded with victim-blaming rhetoric and dehumanizing celebrations, underscoring the deep divisions that Charlie fought so hard to bridge. There message is clear, keep different points of views to yourself or else.
Above all, Charlie Kirk was a man of deep faith who loved our Savior Jesus Christ, cherished his family above everything, and was passionately devoted to our country. He courageously pushed back against the radical left's divisive ideologies that have eroded our national unity. His life was cut short for simply exercising his First Amendment rights, a stark reminder of the fragility of our freedoms.
Rage is Not the Only Answer
In stark contrast to the unrest we've seen in other tragedies, Charlie's supporters are responding with mourning and resolve, not riots, looting, or destruction. Let us honor his example by committing to the civility he championed.
Charlie Kirk's funeral will be held next Sunday, September 21, in Arizona, where friends, family, and admirers from across the nation will gather to celebrate a life that inspired millions. In this time of sorrow, may we rededicate ourselves to the values Charlie held dear: faith, family, freedom, and fearless truth-telling. Our thoughts and prayers remain with Erika and the children, may God's peace surround them.
[Visit the Turning Point USA website here to learn more and get involved in the movement.](https://eky6fg9ab.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001J7c7RgG4dlJKcalE8QKO2JKldwrpx5C8y0Ux8aVhCnerFYECc29Zpx4Ok-RCPlwAehOXKktD8DFESKz5ggIypEMJDwAA9YOm_NC-KdlMdikBUjsDo-pzfBZ5rhuLTwTeAJGy1HTBwSu0IYiB18Gt9Q==&c=7-e-QeJb7bAcDVKZdNQISY-fTHz-E1HBY0ViUJTdu8ILbBKXhbI66Q==&ch=5JTD4agVPIuppVWzbKBa69obBIz5IvcyFNPlw3l7G36DwuPxzekUsA==)

| Erika Kirk delivered a moving and heartfelt message about her deep love for her remarkable husband, Charlie Kirk. He had the potential to be an extraordinary president of the United States one day. Tragically, Charlie's children will grow up without their father. Though this loss is profound, I trust that God will bring good from this sorrow, even in the midst of a difficult week. Charlie's unwavering commitment to loving Jesus Christ and upholding the founding principles of our nation will only grow stronger, inspiring countless others to carry his vision forward.
My prayer is that his legacy and message will continue to draw our country closer to God, fostering a brighter future for all Americans. [Click here](https://eky6fg9ab.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001J7c7RgG4dlJKcalE8QKO2JKldwrpx5C8y0Ux8aVhCnerFYECc29Zpx4Ok-RCPlwAfEITvy3jelyvxlUJC5mTbL0IsbYbBGupQbcHtTcKSYCdjDhLnGXGCGbXaFnKfaFWCNHpSxEFafGZDyKk1ysqkyL9MZdOo-TMs-xOc23R2FI7sLqyHXz5Xg==&c=7-e-QeJb7bAcDVKZdNQISY-fTHz-E1HBY0ViUJTdu8ILbBKXhbI66Q==&ch=5JTD4agVPIuppVWzbKBa69obBIz5IvcyFNPlw3l7G36DwuPxzekUsA==)

| 5 Days Until Early Voting
---
| |
---
| Common Sense Over Nonsense
---
| Team,
Early voting begins in just 5 days, on September 19th! Let's spread the word to friends, family, and neighbors to vote Republican and support our shared values of God, Family, the Constitution, Freedom, a strong business climate, and common-sense policies.
Recently, every House Democrat in Washington, D.C., voted against the Working Family Tax Cut Act, a bill that would have prevented the largest tax increase on the middle class in history, supported law and order, codified 28 Trump Executive Orders, promoted safe cities, and fueled economic growth.
Instead, Democrats, led by figures like Spanberger, continue to push policies that defy reason and morality, including:

* Allowing boys in girls' bathrooms and sports
* Supporting gender surgeries for minors
* Advocating open borders
* Releasing dangerous criminals onto our streets
* Defunding the police

Our job as legislators is to be your voice and get things done. I've been able to pass bills each year where every republican and every democrat voted yes, but the policies supported by Spanberger stand in stark contrast to our commitment to law-abiding citizens and Christian values. We must reject the normalization of harmful ideas and ask: Why do Democrats prioritize criminals over hardworking families?
Our team has been tirelessly knocking on doors, funding campaigns across Virginia, putting up signs, and greeting voters across the 5th Congressional District. All three statewide offices and all 100 House of Delegates seats are up for grabs this November. Winsome Sears and our full Republican ticket are aligned on the issues that matter, but victory depends on turning out the vote.
For Freedom and Common Sense, I urge you to vote early on September 19th. This will free you up to help us rally others to the polls. Together, we can secure a brighter future for our district and our nation.
Here are some photos from around the district and some of what we have been up to:

Her tireless America First leadership sets the example for other GOP chairs. Ramona and her teams efforts were instrumental in delivering record turnout for President Trump in 2024.
From helping organize every Trump rally in Virginia to championing the entire Republican ticket, Ramona's commitment has never been about "me"; it's always been about "we."
Ramona and her team's example is exactly what we need to secure victory this November.
President Trump, Chris LaCivita, and the people of Virginia's 5th trust in Ramona Christian. For all things political for John McGuire in District 5, please send Ramona Christian an email to ramona@mcguireva.com

| A Nice Birthday Present
---
| |
---
| Pastor Jonathan Falwell
---
| |
---
| |
---
| Sen. Tim Kaine's recent remarks exposed a fundamental truth about the modern Democratic Party: they reject the principle that our inalienable rights are endowed by our Creator.
The United States was founded on the belief that our rights, enshrined in the Constitution, are God-given and inherent to every individual. Yet, Kaine's comments suggest Democrats view these rights as privileges bestowed by the government. This is a profound and dangerous distinction. It reveals a worldview where rights are not sacred but subject to the whims of those in power.
If Democrats believe the government grants our rights, they could just as easily revoke them upon regaining control. This perspective undermines the very foundation of our republic. Our Founding Fathers fought the Revolutionary War to reject tyranny and affirm that our liberties are not contingent on any ruler, regime, or political party.
Our rights are not privileges to be dispensed or withdrawn by a dictator, monarch, or government. They are unalienable, endowed by God, and must be defended as such.
---
|
Thank you for your continued support. Together, we will take our country back. Please ask friends to vote early starting September 19. Take care, please tell a friend, and I look forward to seeing you around the district!

clean_body: | | | | God Bless America! | | | == | == | Champion of Free Speech and Faith Charlie Kirk Assassinated | | | The tragic loss of Charlie Kirk is a profound national tragedy that has left us all heartbroken. At just 31 years old, this devoted father leaves behind his wife, Erika, and their two young children, who will grow up without his guidance simply because he dared to voice a differing opinion. Charlie was peacefully seated on stage at Utah Valley University, engaging in an open debate as part of his "American Comeback Tour," when an assassin's bullet ended his life on September 10, 2025, an act of senseless political violence that has shocked the nation. We have been holding his family in our prayers throughout this devastating week, seeking comfort for their unimaginable grief. Tonight, there will be a community Prayer Vigil in honor of Charlie Kirk at 5 p.m. on the Courthouse Lawn in Goochland County, Virginia. This gathering is an opportunity to come together in unity, reflection, and prayer, echoing the many vigils springing up across the country to remember his legacy. Charlie Kirk was a tireless advocate for free speech, open debate, and civil discourse. Through Turning Point USA, he created platforms where even his fiercest critics could voice opposing views without fear. Yet, in a heartbreaking act of domestic terrorism, he was assassinated by 22-year-old suspect Tyler Robinson, who reportedly harbored disdain for Kirk's conservative viewpoints. Tragically, following his death, many on the radical left have responded with victim-blaming rhetoric and dehumanizing celebrations, underscoring the deep divisions that Charlie fought so hard to bridge. There message is clear, keep different points of views to yourself or else. Above all, Charlie Kirk was a man of deep faith who loved our Savior Jesus Christ, cherished his family above everything, and was passionately devoted to our country. He courageously pushed back against the radical left's divisive ideologies that have eroded our national unity. His life was cut short for simply exercising his First Amendment rights, a stark reminder of the fragility of our freedoms. Rage is Not the Only Answer In stark contrast to the unrest we've seen in other tragedies, Charlie's supporters are responding with mourning and resolve, not riots, looting, or destruction. Let us honor his example by committing to the civility he championed. Charlie Kirk's funeral will be held next Sunday, September 21, in Arizona, where friends, family, and admirers from across the nation will gather to celebrate a life that inspired millions. In this time of sorrow, may we rededicate ourselves to the values Charlie held dear: faith, family, freedom, and fearless truth-telling. Our thoughts and prayers remain with Erika and the children, may God's peace surround them. == | Evil Will Not Win, The Movement Will Go On | | == | Erika Kirk delivered a moving and heartfelt message about her deep love for her remarkable husband, Charlie Kirk. He had the potential to be an extraordinary president of the United States one day. Tragically, Charlie's children will grow up without their father. Though this loss is profound, I trust that God will bring good from this sorrow, even in the midst of a difficult week. Charlie's unwavering commitment to loving Jesus Christ and upholding the founding principles of our nation will only grow stronger, inspiring countless others to carry his vision forward. My prayer is that his legacy and message will continue to draw our country closer to God, fostering a brighter future for all Americans. == | 5 Days Until Early Voting | | | Common Sense Over Nonsense | Team, Early voting begins in just 5 days, on September 19th! Let's spread the word to friends, family, and neighbors to vote Republican and support our shared values of God, Family, the Constitution, Freedom, a strong business climate, and common-sense policies. Recently, every House Democrat in Washington, D.C., voted against the Working Family Tax Cut Act, a bill that would have prevented the largest tax increase on the middle class in history, supported law and order, codified 28 Trump Executive Orders, promoted safe cities, and fueled economic growth. Instead, Democrats, led by figures like Spanberger, continue to push policies that defy reason and morality, including: Allowing boys in girls' bathrooms and sports Supporting gender surgeries for minors Advocating open borders Releasing dangerous criminals onto our streets * Defunding the police Our job as legislators is to be your voice and get things done. I've been able to pass bills each year where every republican and every democrat voted yes, but the policies supported by Spanberger stand in stark contrast to our commitment to law-abiding citizens and Christian values. We must reject the normalization of harmful ideas and ask: Why do Democrats prioritize criminals over hardworking families? Our team has been tirelessly knocking on doors, funding campaigns across Virginia, putting up signs, and greeting voters across the 5th Congressional District. All three statewide offices and all 100 House of Delegates seats are up for grabs this November. Winsome Sears and our full Republican ticket are aligned on the issues that matter, but victory depends on turning out the vote. For Freedom and Common Sense, I urge you to vote early on September 19th. This will free you up to help us rally others to the polls. Together, we can secure a brighter future for our district and our nation. Here are some photos from around the district and some of what we have been up to: | First White House Rose Garden Club Dinner | | Thank you President Trump for working tirelessly to Make America Great Again for all Americans! Fellow Veteran and Vice President JD Vance | | Speaker Mike Johnson | | | The Power of Prayer, Thank you Patricia! | | | Cumberland County | | It is an honor to meet folks around the 5th Congressional District. | Re-elect Delegate Chris Obenshain | | | Fellow Congressman Morgan Griffith and I attended an event to show support for Delegate Chris Obenshain's for re-election. Please remind family and friends early voting begins September 19. | Team McGuire Political Director Ramona Christian | | | A heartfelt thank you, to a very accomplished person, GOP Chair Ramona Christian for her unwavering dedication to Buckingham County, Virginia's 5th Congressional District, our Republican candidates, and President Trump. Her tireless America First leadership sets the example for other GOP chairs. Ramona and her teams efforts were instrumental in delivering record turnout for President Trump in 2024. From helping organize every Trump rally in Virginia to championing the entire Republican ticket, Ramona's commitment has never been about "me"; it's always been about "we." Ramona and her team's example is exactly what we need to secure victory this November. President Trump, Chris LaCivita, and the people of Virginia's 5th trust in Ramona Christian. For all things political for John McGuire in District 5, please send Ramona Christian an email to ramona@mcguireva.com | A Nice Birthday Present | | | Pastor Jonathan Falwell | | | | | Sen. Tim Kaine's recent remarks exposed a fundamental truth about the modern Democratic Party: they reject the principle that our inalienable rights are endowed by our Creator. The United States was founded on the belief that our rights, enshrined in the Constitution, are God-given and inherent to every individual. Yet, Kaine's comments suggest Democrats view these rights as privileges bestowed by the government. This is a profound and dangerous distinction. It reveals a worldview where rights are not sacred but subject to the whims of those in power. If Democrats believe the government grants our rights, they could just as easily revoke them upon regaining control. This perspective undermines the very foundation of our republic. Our Founding Fathers fought the Revolutionary War to reject tyranny and affirm that our liberties are not contingent on any ruler, regime, or political party. Our rights are not privileges to be dispensed or withdrawn by a dictator, monarch, or government. They are unalienable, endowed by God, and must be defended as such. | Thank you for your continued support. Together, we will take our country back. Please ask friends to vote early starting September 19. Take care, please tell a friend, and I look forward to seeing you around the district! == == == | For God and Country, | Congressman John McGuire VA 05 Soli Deo Gloria Jesus is my Savior Husband Father Navy SEAL Business Owner -Trump Endorsed -NRA Endorsed -Susan B. Anthony's List Endorsed -RJC Endorsed -Mark Levin Endorsed Pro-Life Pro-Gun Pro-Israel American Legion Member VFW (Life Member) NRA (Life Member) Highest Award for Conservatism (ACU/ CPAC) Family Foundation 100 VCDL 100 (Life Member) America First The Spirit of Virginia == our Facebook Page Twitter: @JohnMcGuire4VA Instagram: JohnMcGuire4VA == == john@mcguireva.com www.mcguireva.com | | | | == | | == | == | == == == | Paid for and Authorized by McGuire for Virginia | | | | McGuire for Virginia | PO Box 242 | Manakin Sabot, VA 23103 US | | | | | | |

# [Seeing the Humanity in the People We Disagree With](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4399133&post_id=173847077&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTczODQ3MDc3LCJpYXQiOjE3NTgxMTQyNjMsImV4cCI6MTc2MDcwNjI2MywiaXNzIjoicHViLTQzOTkxMzMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.uWuPatn18E54Se-FmEmrY8njUzRmqng5by5pZdpWSTo)
### What the murder of Charlie Kirk reveals about America's capacity for empathy across party lines.
| | [Patrick J. Murphy](https://substack.com/@patrickmurphypa)
---
| Sep 17
---
| [](https://substack.com/@patrickmurphypa)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4399133&post_id=173847077&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTczODQ3MDc3LCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc1ODExNDI2MywiZXhwIjoxNzYwNzA2MjYzLCJpc3MiOiJwdWItNDM5OTEzMyIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.-nIDF422nhUdJXhybC5g8g6JvfkLTHETlxyLQjHIqu0&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4399133&post_id=173847077&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTczODQ3MDc3LCJpYXQiOjE3NTgxMTQyNjMsImV4cCI6MTc2MDcwNjI2MywiaXNzIjoicHViLTQzOTkxMzMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.uWuPatn18E54Se-FmEmrY8njUzRmqng5by5pZdpWSTo&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4399133&post_id=173847077&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTczODQ3MDc3LCJpYXQiOjE3NTgxMTQyNjMsImV4cCI6MTc2MDcwNjI2MywiaXNzIjoicHViLTQzOTkxMzMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.uWuPatn18E54Se-FmEmrY8njUzRmqng5by5pZdpWSTo)

| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/patrickmurphypa/p/seeing-the-humanity-in-the-people?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

It's been a week since the murder of Charlie Kirk. As [I wrote last week](https://substack.com/redirect/a2f546a6-3a13-407c-bb50-9aa6dea32990?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), this is a tragedy that should unite the country. Instead, it further divided us politically. As if we needed another reason to tear each other apart.
In today's America, saying that someone shouldn't be gunned down sparks outrage when that person was controversial and highly objectionable to a large portion of the country.
Mourning someone I disagreed with is not weakness. It's strength. Every human life has dignity. Every human life has worth. If we forget that, we forget ourselves.. A person isn't just worthy based on how closely their political ideology aligns with your own.
Some of you wrote me supportive notes, while others took to the comments in a fury.
I welcome that feedback, though. Truly. Because if we can't even talk across lines of difference anymore, then the whole idea of America starts to fray.
So let's talk.
Charlie Kirk was exercising his freedom of speech, guaranteed by the First Amendment of our Constitution. Whether you loved his arguments or loathed them, whether you found him dangerous or courageous, the fact remains: he stood on campuses and said to anyone who would listen, _prove me wrong._
Open debate and intellectual sparring are a cornerstone of American democracy. While Kirk may have been abrasive at times in the way he engaged, he deserves some credit for showing up in spaces where he knew his views wouldn't be welcome. We need more dialogue, not less. As _New York Times_ columnist Ezra Klein wrote last week, [Charlie Kirk was practicing politics the right way.](https://substack.com/redirect/a5a50867-8067-4d6b-a033-1b95246b130f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) He understood the art of persuasion.
If my writing resonates with you, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..ZLIDE7wPhHiv-uaRWAoEEqtKi1zZ9FEYRvdLnviTLNk?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=173847077)
***
Democratic Senator Chris Coons, who [joined us recently on the podcast,](https://substack.com/@patrickmurphypa/p-172895477) exemplified the best of pluralism on[ ](https://substack.com/redirect/a2343058-abdf-4148-b71c-0ba39caf4777?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_[Face the Nation](https://substack.com/redirect/a2343058-abdf-4148-b71c-0ba39caf4777?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_ in a conversation with his conservative Republican colleague James Lankford. The two disagreed on almost everything, yet sat side-by-side, modeling the civic friendship that makes a democracy work.
This is the part we're losing. And if we lose that, we lose everything.
# What the Army Taught Me
When I served in the Army, I learned something fundamental: when you're uncertain, you return to the basics. You double down on what keeps you grounded.
In order to be in an elite unit, like the 82nd Airborne Division, that meant developing and maintaining "winning habits." I'm talking 82 pushups. A sub-12-minute two-mile run. A twelve-mile ruck march under three hours. Jumping out of an airplane with your M-4 rifle and fifty pounds of gear. They were the blueprint for discipline, excellence, and trust.
For our democracy, the blueprint is just as clear: the Constitution.
I recently spoke with Blake Hall, CEO & co-founder of ID.me, on my [Yahoo Finance Warrior Money Podcas](https://substack.com/redirect/b6d1b9b1-28aa-427e-a5e6-85c65b799034?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)t. We broke down the habits that fuel winning and how excellence becomes second nature through consistency.
[patrickmurphypa](https://substack.com/redirect/d156fa63-58c7-42ee-8436-d9a6fbe05826?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
[](https://substack.com/redirect/576ba9ff-b307-495b-b78c-512b9afee2df?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
A post shared by [@patrickmurphypa](https://substack.com/redirect/d156fa63-58c7-42ee-8436-d9a6fbe05826?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
# Read the Constitution
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/0f22df81-a5bc-4100-9b53-ca8945bb7dc2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Source: [Intrepid Fallen Heroes Fund](https://substack.com/redirect/68b8e982-0bd5-47e4-9bc8-cf2406309b1d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
How do we cultivate habits that improve our democracy in a society that is increasingly fragmented? I'd suggest returning to our founding document--which is fitting since **today is National Constitution Day.**
I spent years teaching the Constitution at West Point. It was the final core class every cadet took before commissioning into the Army--a reminder that before you go out to lead soldiers, you need to understand the document you're sworn to support and defend.
The Constitution isn't just ancient parchment. It's a living, breathing covenant. It's been amended 27 times. And right there in the preamble, it admits we weren't perfect--but that we would always strive "to form a more perfect union."
When West Point's Class of 1944 graduated early to rush into combat in World War II, the grads who came home pooled their money together for a class gift: it became Constitution Corner, a reminder that even in war, what they fought for was not land or treasure, but an idea.
In essence, the U.S. Constitution holds the answers to our "winning habits." That's our North Star. It lays out our individual rights and fundamental principles of justice, equality, and fundamental freedoms granted by our creator, while also establishing a limited government by establishing three co-equal branches of government: executive, legislative and judicial, enumerating the powers of each and establishing the checks and balances needed to ensure our democracy lasts (similar to how how the simple game of rock, paper, scissor works).
# Bipartisanship on 9/11
Last week, when speaking at the Bush Library on the anniversary of 9/11, veteran leaders from across the nation gathered--men and women who know what it means to carry the weight of leadership in impossible times.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/c8108d5c-8815-46a4-8f99-12b82807a8a4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
With fellow Iraq war vets and 82nd Airborne paratroopers, Juan Arevalo and Jenny Williamson, standing in front of World Trade Center steel beams at the President Bush Library.
I reminded them of something Shakespeare wrote in _Henry IV Part 2_ : "Heavy is the head that wears the crown." If you want to be a leader, you have to get used to discomfort. Leadership comes with burden and sacrifice. It's showing up when you'd rather sit out.
General Mark Milley, the former Chairman of the Joint Chiefs, once told me something similar when we filmed with him at the National Constitution Center before the Army-Navy Game. Leadership, he said, means serving something bigger than yourself--the people, the nation, the democratic promise.
That's what America is asking of us now.
So here's the question I'll leave you with: Are you willing to lead?
Leadership doesn't always mean holding elected office or commanding troops. It might mean listening without demonizing. It might mean asking a question instead of lobbing an insult. It might mean setting aside pride long enough to genuinely think: _maybe the other side has a point._
It's easy to give in to cynicism. Easy to shut down. Easy to decide it's not worth the fight. But that's not what God--or America--calls us to do.
The Constitution is not just ink on paper. It is a set of habits we must practice, especially when it's hardest.
Heavy is the head that wears the crown. But the crown belongs to each of us. Every citizen. Every voter. Every voice.
The only question is: will we carry it with the grit and grace needed during these defining moments?

clean_body: What the murder of Charlie Kirk reveals about America's capacity for empathy across party lines. | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more ? Thank you for supporting my work. If my writing resonates with you, consider . Annual paid subscribers will receive a signed copy of my 2008 memoir, What the murder of Charlie Kirk reveals about America's capacity for empathy across party lines. | | | Sep 17 | | | | | | | | | | | | It's been a week since the murder of Charlie Kirk. As , this is a tragedy that should unite the country. Instead, it further divided us politically. As if we needed another reason to tear each other apart. In today's America, saying that someone shouldn't be gunned down sparks outrage when that person was controversial and highly objectionable to a large portion of the country. Mourning someone I disagreed with is not weakness. It's strength. Every human life has dignity. Every human life has worth. If we forget that, we forget ourselves.. A person isn't just worthy based on how closely their political ideology aligns with your own. Some of you wrote me supportive notes, while others took to the comments in a fury. I welcome that feedback, though. Truly. Because if we can't even talk across lines of difference anymore, then the whole idea of America starts to fray. So let's talk. Charlie Kirk was exercising his freedom of speech, guaranteed by the First Amendment of our Constitution. Whether you loved his arguments or loathed them, whether you found him dangerous or courageous, the fact remains: he stood on campuses and said to anyone who would listen, prove me wrong. Open debate and intellectual sparring are a cornerstone of American democracy. While Kirk may have been abrasive at times in the way he engaged, he deserves some credit for showing up in spaces where he knew his views wouldn't be welcome. We need more dialogue, not less. As New York Times columnist Ezra Klein wrote last week, He understood the art of persuasion. If my writing resonates with you, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber. Democratic Senator Chris Coons, who exemplified the best of pluralism on in a conversation with his conservative Republican colleague James Lankford. The two disagreed on almost everything, yet sat side-by-side, modeling the civic friendship that makes a democracy work. This is the part we're losing. And if we lose that, we lose everything. What the Army Taught Me When I served in the Army, I learned something fundamental: when you're uncertain, you return to the basics. You double down on what keeps you grounded. In order to be in an elite unit, like the 82nd Airborne Division, that meant developing and maintaining "winning habits." I'm talking 82 pushups. A sub-12-minute two-mile run. A twelve-mile ruck march under three hours. Jumping out of an airplane with your M-4 rifle and fifty pounds of gear. They were the blueprint for discipline, excellence, and trust. For our democracy, the blueprint is just as clear: the Constitution. I recently spoke with Blake Hall, CEO & co-founder of ID.me, on my . We broke down the habits that fuel winning and how excellence becomes second nature through consistency. A post shared by Read the Constitution | | ---|---|--- Source: How do we cultivate habits that improve our democracy in a society that is increasingly fragmented? I'd suggest returning to our founding document--which is fitting since today is National Constitution Day.** I spent years teaching the Constitution at West Point. It was the final core class every cadet took before commissioning into the Army--a reminder that before you go out to lead soldiers, you need to understand the document you're sworn to support and defend. The Constitution isn't just ancient parchment. It's a living, breathing covenant. It's been amended 27 times. And right there in the preamble, it admits we weren't perfect--but that we would always strive "to form a more perfect union." When West Point's Class of 1944 graduated early to rush into combat in World War II, the grads who came home pooled their money together for a class gift: it became Constitution Corner, a reminder that even in war, what they fought for was not land or treasure, but an idea. In essence, the U.S. Constitution holds the answers to our "winning habits." That's our North Star. It lays out our individual rights and fundamental principles of justice, equality, and fundamental freedoms granted by our creator, while also establishing a limited government by establishing three co-equal branches of government: executive, legislative and judicial, enumerating the powers of each and establishing the checks and balances needed to ensure our democracy lasts (similar to how how the simple game of rock, paper, scissor works). Bipartisanship on 9/11 Last week, when speaking at the Bush Library on the anniversary of 9/11, veteran leaders from across the nation gathered--men and women who know what it means to carry the weight of leadership in impossible times. | | ---|---|--- With fellow Iraq war vets and 82nd Airborne paratroopers, Juan Arevalo and Jenny Williamson, standing in front of World Trade Center steel beams at the President Bush Library. I reminded them of something Shakespeare wrote in Henry IV Part 2 : "Heavy is the head that wears the crown." If you want to be a leader, you have to get used to discomfort. Leadership comes with burden and sacrifice. It's showing up when you'd rather sit out. General Mark Milley, the former Chairman of the Joint Chiefs, once told me something similar when we filmed with him at the National Constitution Center before the Army-Navy Game. Leadership, he said, means serving something bigger than yourself--the people, the nation, the democratic promise. That's what America is asking of us now. So here's the question I'll leave you with: Are you willing to lead? Leadership doesn't always mean holding elected office or commanding troops. It might mean listening without demonizing. It might mean asking a question instead of lobbing an insult. It might mean setting aside pride long enough to genuinely think: maybe the other side has a point. It's easy to give in to cynicism. Easy to shut down. Easy to decide it's not worth the fight. But that's not what God--or America--calls us to do. The Constitution is not just ink on paper. It is a set of habits we must practice, especially when it's hardest. Heavy is the head that wears the crown. But the crown belongs to each of us. Every citizen. Every voter. Every voice. The only question is: will we carry it with the grit and grace needed during these defining moments? If my writing resonates with you, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber.
